description: Graph build tests for structures with well-known coordination geometries

assertions.description: Sr should have CN=12 (cuboctahedral, ABO3 A-site)
Ti should have CN=6 (octahedral, ABO3 B-site)
O should have CN=6 (2 Ti + 4 Sr in cubic perovskite)

tests.description: SrTiO3 cubic perovskite: total nodes and edges for primitive cell

assertions.description: Primitive cell has 5 sites (1 Sr + 1 Ti + 3 O)
18 edges expected (12 Sr-O + 6 Ti-O in the primitive cell)

tests.description: SrTiO3 cubic perovskite: corner-sharing TiO6 octahedra; Sr-Ti face-sharing

assertions.description: Each TiO6 corner-shares with 6 other TiO6 (all 6 octahedral neighbors via corner-linked network)
Each SrO12 face-shares with 8 TiO6 (A-B interface: each cuboctahedron has 8 triangular faces shared with octahedra)
Ti should have CN=6 (octahedral in ilmenite)
Fe should have CN=6 (octahedral in ilmenite)
O should have CN=4 in ilmenite corundum-type structure

tests.description: TiFeO3 ilmenite: face- and edge-sharing between Ti and Fe octahedra (corundum-type)

assertions.description: Each TiO6 face-shares with 1 FeO6 (defining feature of ilmenite)
Each TiO6 edge-shares with 3 FeO6 (secondary corundum-type connection)
Each TiO6 corner-shares with 3 FeO6 (long-range inter-layer connection beyond the face- and edge-sharing pair)
Each TiO6 corner-shares with 6 other TiO6 (NOTE: textbook 'edge-sharing within Ti layers' refers to hexagonal supercell, not primitive R-3)
Each FeO6 corner-shares with 6 other FeO6 (same reason as Ti-Ti)

tests.description: NaCl rocksalt (Fm-3m): octahedral coordination for both ions, prototype ionic structure

assertions.description: Na should have CN=6 (octahedral in rocksalt)
Cl should have CN=6 (octahedral in rocksalt)
Each NaCl6 corner-shares with 6 other NaCl6 (next-nearest-neighbor Na through Cl)
Each NaCl6 edge-shares with 12 other NaCl6 (nearest-neighbor Na pairs share 2 Cl — the dominant connectivity in rocksalt)

tests.description: TiO2 rutile (P42/mnm): octahedral Ti, trigonal O; edge-sharing chains

assertions.description: Ti should have CN=6 (octahedral in rutile)
O should have CN=3 (trigonal, bonded to 3 Ti in rutile)
Each TiO6 edge-shares with 2 other TiO6 along the c-axis chain (1 above, 1 below)
Each TiO6 corner-shares with 8 other TiO6 between chains (4-fold connectivity in the ab-plane plus 4 diagonal)

tests.description: Al2O3 corundum (R-3c): octahedral Al, 4-coordinate O; face+edge sharing

assertions.description: Al should have CN=6 (octahedral in corundum)
O should have CN=4 (bonded to 4 Al in corundum)
Each AlO6 face-shares with 1 other AlO6 (along c — the corundum face-sharing pair)
Each AlO6 edge-shares with 3 in-plane AlO6 neighbors (corundum honeycomb layer)
Each AlO6 corner-shares with 9 other AlO6 (longer-range connections beyond face and edge sharing)

tests.description: Cr2O3 corundum (R-3c): same topology as Al2O3; validates that different chemistry gives identical connectivity fingerprint

assertions.description: Cr should have CN=6 (octahedral in corundum)
O should have CN=4
Each CrO6 face-shares with 1 other CrO6 (same corundum motif as Al2O3)
Each CrO6 edge-shares with 3 in-plane CrO6 neighbors (corundum honeycomb layer)
Each CrO6 corner-shares with 9 other CrO6 (identical to Al2O3 corundum topology)

tests.description: MgAl2O4 normal spinel (Fd-3m): tetrahedral Mg (A-site) and octahedral Al (B-site) — mixed coordination

assertions.description: Mg should have CN=4 (tetrahedral A-site in normal spinel)
Al should have CN=6 (octahedral B-site in normal spinel)
Each AlO6 edge-shares with 6 other AlO6 (B-site octahedral chains in spinel)
Each MgO4 tetrahedron corner-shares with 6 AlO6 octahedra (A-B site connectivity in normal spinel)

tests.description: ReO3 (Pm-3m): fully corner-connected ReO6 network — the perovskite B-site sublattice without an A-site

assertions.description: Re should have CN=6 (corner-linked octahedral network)
O should have CN=2 (bridging between exactly 2 Re octahedra)
Each ReO6 corner-shares with 6 other ReO6 (fully corner-connected cubic network — same count as Ti-Ti in SrTiO3)

tests.description: BaZrO3 cubic perovskite (Pm-3m): Ba/Zr chemistry variant of SrTiO3; validates CN assignment for larger A-site cation

assertions.description: Ba should have CN=12 (cuboctahedral A-site)
Zr should have CN=6 (octahedral B-site)
Each ZrO6 corner-shares with 6 other ZrO6 (same topology as SrTiO3 Ti-Ti)
Each BaO12 face-shares with 8 ZrO6 (same A-B count as SrTiO3)

tests.description: LaFeO3 GdFeO3-type distorted perovskite (Pbnm): tilted FeO6 octahedra; La A-site remains ~12-coordinate

assertions.description: La should have CN≈12 (tolerance 2 for GdFeO3-type distortion which splits the 12-fold site)
Fe should have CN=6 (octahedral; preserved through the tilt transition)
Each FeO6 corner-shares with 6 other FeO6 despite the tilt (GdFeO3-type is a=a-c+ tilt, not a connectivity change)
Each FeO6 face-shares with 8 LaO12 polyhedra (A-B face-sharing preserved through the GdFeO3-type tilt, same count as cubic perovskite)

tests.description: LiCoO2 layered oxide (R-3m, alpha-NaFeO2 type): edge-sharing CoO6 slabs alternating with LiO6 slabs

assertions.description: Co should have CN=6 (octahedral in the Co slab)
Li should have CN=6 (octahedral in the Li slab)
Each CoO6 edge-shares with 6 other CoO6 within the Co triangular layer
Each LiO6 edge-shares with 6 CoO6 across the interlayer boundary (rock-salt derived — no face-sharing)
Each LiO6 edge-shares with 6 other LiO6 within the Li triangular layer (same topology as Co-Co)
Each CoO6 corner-shares with 6 LiO6 (longer-range interlayer connection beyond the edge-sharing pair)

tests.description: MgTiO3 ilmenite (R-3): non-transition-metal A-site variant; validates ilmenite detection with Mg at the Fe position

assertions.description: Mg should have CN=6 (octahedral, like Fe in FeTiO3)
Ti should have CN=6 (octahedral)
O should have CN=4 (corundum-type)
Each MgO6 face-shares with 1 TiO6 (the defining ilmenite motif — same as Fe-Ti in TiFeO3)
Each MgO6 corner-shares with 9 TiO6 (3 to the face-sharing Ti partner + 6 to the opposite Ti layer; no edge-sharing between unlike cations in MgTiO3 unlike FeTiO3)
Each MgO6 edge-shares with 3 other MgO6 (within the Mg honeycomb layer, same as Al-Al in corundum)
Each TiO6 edge-shares with 3 other TiO6 (within the Ti honeycomb layer)

tests.description: Fe3O4 inverse spinel (Fd-3m): Fe occupies both tetrahedral (CN=4) and octahedral (CN=6) sites — tests any_node_cn for mixed coordination

assertions.description: At least one Fe node should have CN=4 (tetrahedral A-site in inverse spinel, occupied by Fe3+)
At least one Fe node should have CN=6 (octahedral B-site in inverse spinel, mixed Fe2+/Fe3+)
Octahedral Fe-Fe edge-sharing (B-site chains, characteristic of spinel B sublattice)

tests.description: CoBiO3 tetragonal perovskite (mp-545522, P1 primitive cell): Co at B-site (CN=6), Bi at A-site (CN=12); validates perovskite detection with heavy A-site cation

assertions.description: Co should have CN=6 (octahedral B-site)
Bi should have CN=12 (cuboctahedral A-site)
O should have CN=6 (2 Co + 4 Bi neighbours, same as SrTiO3 oxygen)
Each CoO6 corner-shares with 6 other CoO6 (same corner-linked octahedral network as TiO6 in SrTiO3)
Each CoO6 face-shares with 8 BiO12 polyhedra (A-B interface; same count as Ti-Sr in SrTiO3)
